title: An Emu Is Loose in New Jersey. Nobody Knows Who Lost It.
script: New Jersey has an emu problem! The bird showed up in Byram Township, Sussex County this week — large, fluffy, and completely unaccounted for. Animal control officers, dog handlers, and large-bird wranglers have all tried to catch it. None of them succeeded. The emu was spotted on International Drive in Budd Lake, then immediately made itself scarce. Animal shelters posted on Facebook. Police departments asked the public for help. Nobody has come forward to claim it. Here's the thing about emus: they run 31 miles per hour. So when an emu doesn't want to be caught, it won't be caught. As of this morning, it's still out there. So who exactly lost a 130-pound bird in New Jersey and just... forgot?
